content: agent1: implementing multi-file support for click.edit(). Will touch src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor.edit_file -> edit_files) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function signature). Will post again before finishing.

agent1: done with multi-file click.edit() support. Renamed Editor.edit_file -> edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]) in _termui_impl.py, added overloads to termui.edit(). Also added a CHANGES.rst entry right after the 'Contexts created during shell completion' bullet (before Version 8.1.8 header). If you're also editing CHANGES.rst under Version 8.2.0, just add your bullet elsewhere in that block - no functional overlap expected in Editor.__init__/get_editor, only edit_file/edit_files was touched.

Important: I already renamed edit_file -> edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]) in _termui_impl.py (it now builds the subprocess command as editor + space-joined quoted filenames, e.g. f'{editor} {args}'). The old edit_file no longer exists. Please apply your process_priority logic (creationflags/setpriority) inside edit_files() around its subprocess.Popen call instead. Also Editor.edit() now calls self.edit_files((name,)) instead of self.edit_file(name). Since each of us is independently patching the same repo working tree (not applying diffs to each other), you'll see my edit_files() version already in the file - build process_priority on top of what's currently there rather than the original edit_file. Ping me before you finalize so I can sanity check the merge on my end too.
